name: Necesse
short_description: Build, quest, and conquer across an infinite procedurally generated world. Play alone or with friends as you establish a settlement and explore deep dungeons, fight monsters and bosses, mine rare ores, craft magical equipment, recruit specialists for your colony, and more!
detailed_description:
Roadmap


About the Game


Build. Quest. Conquer. The infinite procedurally generated world of Necesse is full of mysteries to uncover as you grow your settlement and lead expeditions against pirate kings, dragons, and fallen wizards alike. Will you spend your days crawling through dungeons, searching for treasures and raw materials with which to craft your equipment? Or will you answer the call to adventure, traversing the seas and seeking out great foes to test your prowess? Recruit settlers with particular skills and work alongside them to mine a wide variety of ores, craft gear tailored to your preferred playstyle, and establish a flourishing village.

Play singleplayer or co-op - or establish a dedicated server with hundreds of other players. The world is infinite and adapts to whatever mode you choose.


Grow your settlement from humble beginnings, establishing orchards, farms, and workshops alike. Recruit settlers that can work your fields, tend to your animals, and acquire resources without needing your constant attention and supervision. Use their expertise to help prepare for your next expedition as they expand your settlement's capabilities. Hire guards and craft traps to defend against the many dangers of the world. Sell your wares and use your wealth to trade with neighbouring settlements, acquiring much-needed equipment well ahead of what would otherwise be possible.


Whether playing alone or with a few friends, or even creating a dedicated server for hundreds of players, the world of Necesse and its threats adapts to provide a meaningful challenge. Explore freely or take on specific quests as you face off against unique monsters spread across a variety of biomes. Will you be a deadeye archer, a melee champion, or a master of the magical arts? Will you and your friends establish separate competing colonies, or will you pool your resources and divide roles to build a giant settlement together? When faced with a tough boss, will you team up to form a carefully balanced party with powerful synergies, or will you attempt to steal all the glory for yourself?


Cut down trees, mine and process ores, pick herbs, and harvest the bodies of defeated monsters to craft everything from walls and furniture to armour, potions, scrolls, trinkets, and weapons. While some unique equipment can only be acquired by defeating special enemies, everything else can be crafted through a multitude of workshops to equip yourself, your visiting friends, and your loyal settlers. Unlock additional gear as you acquire new resources and discover countless ways to craft, build, and play – endless combinations of equipment mean there's always something new to try.

Play as you want, customising difficulty and multiple gameplay rules to fit your preferences. If you don’t like raiders showing up at your settlement, turn them off, and fine-tune gameplay rules like the length of the day/night cycle or the difficulty of boss battles however you like. From pet parrots and penguins to genie lamps, ostrich mounts, and unique outfits, Necesse has countless options to explore. Whether you're playing singleplayer or with friends, and whether you’re cranking up the difficulty or taking it easy, each session is an opportunity to weave an epic tale unlike any that came before it.

supported_languages: English, Russian, Portuguese - Brazil, German, Simplified Chinese, Czech, Japanese, Spanish - Spain, Spanish - Latin America, Traditional Chinese, French, Hungarian, Korean, Turkish, Ukrainian, Italian, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, Norwegian, Polish, Thai

reviews.review_text:
I was so close to whole heartedly recommending this game, and then I ran into a late game boss, found a difficulty spike via a bad game design choice that is not appropriate at all for this kind of game, saw that there were ways to cheese it but also that things only got worse from there and gave up to preserve my blood pressure. (Clarifying, the boss in question is the infamous one that shows up in the Deep Desert Caves.)

Necesse is a great game for a lot of playtime, but I can't recommend it when it goes two-faced at the worst possible moment. Difficulty in real time games isn't a problem for me (I got very far into Monster Hunter World a while back and Titanfall 2 multiplayer was my bread and butter for years), but in a game like this, demanding a perfect build and pixel perfect movement in a game that was downright cozy up to that point is unacceptable. I bought this for the fun rimworld/minecraft style overworld and boss fights up to that point that are well thought out and give you the freedom to beat them how you like. I did not buy this game to run into a boss that punishes you for playing the game any other way than the exact way required to beat it.

(And before anybody asks, I was using a Rifle+Summon build, not melee, and I had killed every prior boss in a single try. I'm also 100% certain that I could eventually figure the current boss out but that's not why I was playing Necesse in the first place.)

Worth the buy if wildly on sale or if you can stomach the gut punch that comes later. Honestly, if you are a masochist or have been pavlov'd by Elden Ring, you will probably like the change in direction that I'm talking about. Otherwise stay away from Necesse. If the dev sees this, I would sincerely recommend backing off the speed that some of the later bosses move at and removing the multihit behavior as both clash wildly with the feel of the game otherwise, and if these elements are things that you think need to be in the game, add difficulty settings so that the people who want them can have them and those of us who play this game to relax don't run into this stuff.
This is a great game to play with friends! <3

Great game, a bit like Terraria but in better.
Amazing game
